posts.author: Kush_Smart
posts.text: The company’s financial position appears comfortable, with a debt-to-equity ratio of around 0.3x and operating cash flow of ₹287 crore compared to net profit of ₹327 crore in FY25. This indicates that profits are converting into cash reasonably well. The increase in working capital is mainly due to higher trade receivables, contract assets, and inventory required to support growth. However, this remains an area to monitor because large amounts of capital are tied up in ongoing projects. The key risks in Transrail are: (1) high dependence on project execution — delays, cost overruns, or executi…
